ranked_movies.title: Children of Men
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed and highly praised for its realistic dystopian world-building, incredible cinematography, emotional impact, and relevance to modern times. Multiple users called it a 'perfect movie' and one of the best dystopian films ever made.

ranked_movies.title: One Battle After Another
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of deep critical analysis and multiple discussion threads. Praised for its political commentary, technical prowess in action sequences, and PTA's masterful blending of serious themes with cartoonish absurdity.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in two separate threads for its mind-blowing and unsettling documentary approach. Described as a film that will severely 'fuck you up' with intention.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a profoundly disturbing and traumatizing film. Notable for its use of infrasonic sound to cause physical discomfort in viewers, adding to its intense impact.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: The original catalyst for the trauma-seeking post. Described as causing panic attacks and nightmares, setting the standard for 'genuinely good movies that fuck you up with intention'.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Multiple recommendations as a film that 'shook me to my core' and is 'extremely dark'. Often mentioned alongside other traumatizing films.

ranked_movies.title: Free Solo
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended twice as a jaw-dropping documentary. Praised for its incredible footage and nerve-wracking viewing experience.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended as a mind-blowing documentary series that completely changes how you see true crime stories.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its beautiful human moments and portrayal of connection, awkwardness, and growth without explosions or twists.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Described as a 'very chilling comedy' that traumatizes viewers with its intersecting storylines and dark themes.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a mind-blowing documentary that reveals an incredible true story, with personal connections from commenters.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a traumatizing film with an ending 'burned in memory'. Focuses on dark themes involving teenagers and HIV.

ranked_movies.title: The Station Agent
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its portrayal of real human moments and early Peter Dinklage performance. Described as a beautiful character study.

ranked_movies.title: Watership Down
ranked_movies.reason: Directly recommended as a film that 'will fuck you the fuck up' in response to the trauma request.

ranked_movies.title: Man on Wire
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a jaw-dropping documentary about an illegal tightrope walk between the WTC towers, described as 'amazing'.

ranked_movies.title: A Clockwork Orange
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that gives nightmares for weeks after viewing, part of a list of psychologically disturbing films.

ranked_movies.title: There's Something Wrong with Aunt Diane
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a fascinating documentary exploring family denial, described as 'one of my favorite documentaries'.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Subject of a detailed analysis thread discussing Jack Torrance's character and Kubrick's filmmaking techniques.

ranked_movies.title: Evil Genius
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a 'crazy shit' documentary about America's most diabolical bank heist.

ranked_movies.title: Jesus Camp
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a jaw-dropping documentary that provides an eye-opening look at religious indoctrination.
